scheme_name: Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for assistance to MSMEs: Assistance in Rent to MSEs
page_title: Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for assistance to MSMEs: Assistance in Rent to MSEs

details: The "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for MSMEs" is an umbrella scheme launched by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat. It aims to empower the state's dynamic MSME sector, which significantly contributes to employment, GDP, and exports. Effective from October 5, 2022, to October 4, 2027, this initiative supports growth, investments, and the vision of "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat" and "Aatmanirbhar Bharat."

The component "Assistance in Rent to MSEs" provides financial assistance to Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) that acquire rented or leased sheds to set up manufacturing activities. The assistance is provided in the form of a reimbursement of the rent paid during the operative period of the scheme.
benefits: Enterprises will receive assistance covering 65% of rent paid, with a maximum limit of ₹1,00,000/- per annum for a 5 years period.
eligibility: Only Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) involved in manufacturing activities are eligible.
The enterprise must have obtained acknowledgment or registration from the Ministry of MSME or the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), as applicable.
The enterprise should have a valid rent deed or lease agreement for the shed used for manufacturing activities.
The manufacturing activity should consume electricity/power for its operations.

online: portal. Click on "New Investor Registration" to access the registration form.
Fill in the Registration Form. Click on "Submit".
Verify the E-mail ID using the link sent on your registered E-mail ID.
After verification, you will be registered successfully.
Enter the email address and password you used during registration.
Click the "Login" button to access your account.
Now fill in the required details and mandatory documents about your business to complete the application process.

documents_required: Copy of Udyam/IEM/LOI Registration certificate.
Copy of the Constitution of the enterprise.
Supporting documents for SC/ST Enterprise.
CA certificate for fixed capital investment.
Copy of Self Certified Share Holding Pattern (If applicable).
Copy of rent / Leased agreement.
Copy of Rent Receipt.
Copy of Lessor’s PAN Card.
Copy of Property Document/ Copy of Index/Copy of sale deed and Copy of the approved plan for the project from Competent Authority/other approvals.
Copy of Electricity Bill.
Undertaking for any assistances from GOG and GOI.
Copy of PAN Card of Enterprise, Promoters/Directors/Partners, authorized person.
Copy of GST registration. (if applicable).
Copy of CA Certified Balance Sheet of the last financial Year/Annual report.
Copy of Bank Statement reflecting payment of Rent.
Bank details for RTGS payment (Cancelled cheque).
Self-Attested documents/ details to be submitted by the Authorized Person.
Copy of Board Resolution/Consent Letter/Power of Attorney.
Declaration for rent Agreement is not between Blood relation/ family member.
Other Documents (if any).

faqs.question: What is the "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for Assistance to MSMEs"?
faqs.answer: The “Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for Assistance to MSMEs” is a state initiative designed to support and strengthen the MSME sector in Gujarat. It aims to enhance growth, encourage investments, and contribute to the vision of self-reliant Gujarat and India.

faqs.question: When is the scheme operative?
faqs.answer: The scheme is operative from October 5, 2022, to October 4, 2027, covering a period of five years.

faqs.question: What are the objectives of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to boost MSME growth, enhance the industrial ecosystem, encourage investments, and contribute to Gujarat’s and India’s self-reliance vision.

faqs.question: What are some initiatives included in the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme includes the establishment of new industrial estates, sector-specific parks, and infrastructure projects, along with amendments to the Gujarat Industrial Policy 2020 to further support MSME growth.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to benefit from the scheme?
faqs.answer: MSMEs established and operating within Gujarat are eligible to benefit from the scheme, as per the criteria defined in the policy framework.

faqs.question: What is the maximum rent assistance an enterprise can receive under this scheme?
faqs.answer: An enterprise can receive up to 65% of the rent paid, with a maximum assistance of ₹1,00,000/- per annum.

faqs.question: For how long can an enterprise avail rent assistance under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The rent assistance is available for a maximum period of 5 years.

faqs.question: Can an enterprise receive rent assistance if they are not currently paying rent?
faqs.answer: No, an enterprise must be actively paying rent for the shed to be eligible for assistance under this scheme.

faqs.question: Is the assistance provided only for manufacturing activities that consume electricity?
faqs.answer: Yes, the assistance is only available for manufacturing activities that consume electricity or power.

faqs.question: Does the enterprise need to prove ownership or possession of the rented shed?
faqs.answer: Yes, the owner of the shed must have legal ownership and possession of the shed to be eligible for rent assistance.

faqs.question: How is the rent assistance paid to the enterprise?
faqs.answer: The assistance is provided as a reimbursement of the rent paid by the enterprise during the operative period of the scheme.

faqs.question: Can an enterprise apply for rent assistance before signing the rent deed?
faqs.answer: No, an enterprise must have a valid rent deed in place before applying for rent assistance.

faqs.question: Is there any support for enterprises that have already completed the rent payment for the entire year?
faqs.answer: Yes, enterprises that have already paid rent during the operative period of the scheme can still claim assistance for the paid rent, provided all eligibility criteria are met.
